metadata.title: Indian Geography - Soils, Natural Vegetation, Forests

questions.question: The Dampier-Hodges line is related to which of the following?
questions.explanation: Dampier-Hodges line is an imaginary line drawn in 1829-1830 to mark the northern boundary of Sundarbans delta. It passes through 24 Parganas South and North districts of West Bengal.

questions.question: Which type of forests occupies the highest percentage of area in India?
questions.explanation: Tropical Deciduous Forests: They are also called the “Monsoon forests”. They grow in the areas with annual rainfall of 70-200 cm. Sandal wood, Shisam, Mahua, Sal, Teak and Bamboo are important species of trees. Moist deciduous and the dry deciduous forests are two major categories of these forests.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a correct statement about Alluvial soil?

options.label: A
options.text: Alluvial soil is most abundant type of soil found in India

options.label: C
options.text: Alluvial soil lacks nitrogen and tend to be phosphoric.

options.label: D
options.text: Alluvial soil generally comprise high percentage of clay and retain moisture for long time

answer.label: D
answer.text: Alluvial soil generally comprise high percentage of clay and retain moisture for long time

questions.explanation: Fourth is not a correct statement.

questions.question: Which among the following is considered to be the best soil for plant growth?
questions.explanation: Loam soil is the mixture of sand, clay and humus. It has good water-retaining capacity and is sufficiently aerated. This soil contains enough mineral salts in it, and suited for growth of vegetation.

questions.question: The word “Mangal” is used for which among the following kinds of vegetations?
questions.explanation: The term Mangal is used for the entire community of organisms in the mangrove forests including plants, fishes and other animals. The term mangrove is used only for plants.
